subject: english
topic_title: Kinds of Pronouns and Rhyme Scheme
learning_objectives: Classify and use different kinds of pronouns

sections.heading: Structure: Kinds of Pronouns
sections.content: A pronoun is a word used instead of a noun. Types include: 1. Personal Pronouns: I, you, he, she, it. 2. Demonstrative Pronouns: this, that, these, those. 3. Interrogative Pronouns: what, which, who, whom. 4. Possessive Pronouns: mine, ours, yours, hers, theirs (note: no apostrophe is used with these).

sections.heading: Speech Work: Rhyme Scheme
sections.content: Rhyme occurs when words end in the same sound. 1. Same vowel ending: go / know. 2. Same final consonants: worst / burst. 3. Same final vowel and consonant: half / laugh. In poetry, rhyme scheme is the pattern of sounds at the ends of lines.
